positions.claim: Fundamental analysis of supply chains and financial metrics is the only valid basis for investment decisions, while technical analysis and chart patterns are actively harmful to decision quality
implicitAssumptions: Price movements reflect information asymmetries that can be exploited through superior fundamental research rather than being efficient aggregations of available information
Chart patterns and technical signals do not contain any predictive information about future price movements that isn't already captured in fundamental analysis
The time horizon for fundamental analysis to be validated by price action is sufficiently short that opportunity cost from ignoring technical momentum is negligible

positions.claim: Memory demand from AI infrastructure represents a structural, insatiable demand that will persist through at least 2028, creating a multi-year supercycle for memory suppliers
implicitAssumptions: AI model training and inference scaling laws will continue to demand exponentially increasing memory capacity faster than manufacturing capacity can expand
Hyperscaler capital allocation to AI infrastructure will remain prioritized over other uses even if early AI product revenues disappoint
Memory technology transitions (HBM generations, new architectures) will not create temporary demand destruction as customers wait for next-generation products

positions.claim: Photonics represents a new architectural paradigm for AI that will create multi-year investment opportunities comparable to the memory supercycle
implicitAssumptions: Current electrical interconnect technology faces fundamental physical limitations that cannot be solved through incremental engineering improvements
NVDA's $4B investment signals a validated technological transition rather than exploratory R&D or strategic hedging
The photonics supply chain has sufficient concentration and barriers to entry that early movers will capture sustained monopolistic returns

positions.claim: Identifying upstream suppliers in complex supply chains (2-3 levels removed from end products) provides systematic alpha because most investors stop at obvious tier-1 suppliers
implicitAssumptions: Bill-of-materials analysis and supply chain mapping are sufficiently difficult or time-consuming that they create durable information advantages
Upstream suppliers capture disproportionate value relative to their revenue contribution when bottlenecks emerge in scaling production
Public disclosure requirements and investor relations practices leave exploitable information gaps about tier-2 and tier-3 supplier relationships

positions.claim: Monopoly or near-monopoly market positions in critical AI infrastructure components justify significantly higher valuations and provide the best risk-adjusted returns
implicitAssumptions: Regulatory or antitrust intervention will not materially constrain pricing power or force technology licensing in AI infrastructure markets
Customer concentration risk (hyperscalers) does not offset monopoly supplier power because hyperscalers cannot credibly threaten backward integration or multi-sourcing
Monopoly positions in narrow technical niches are defensible for multi-year periods despite high returns attracting well-funded competition

positions.claim: High volatility environments (7-10% daily moves, 90%+ IV) create systematic opportunities to profit from options mispricing, particularly through vega exposure in calls
implicitAssumptions: Options market makers systematically misprice volatility in newly-listed leveraged instruments or concentrated markets rather than efficiently incorporating all available information
Realized volatility will exceed implied volatility over the holding period despite IV already being elevated (90%+)
Liquidity conditions (wide spreads) represent temporary mispricing opportunities rather than rational compensation for execution risk and adverse selection

positions.claim: Leveraged instruments (3x ETFs) and leverage during market downturns create unacceptable risk and should be avoided
implicitAssumptions: Volatility decay in leveraged instruments outweighs any potential benefits from amplified exposure during high-conviction opportunities
Market timing ability is insufficient to reliably avoid the catastrophic scenarios where leverage causes permanent capital loss
The psychological and portfolio management challenges of managing leveraged positions during drawdowns override any theoretical edge from fundamental analysis

positions.claim: Geopolitical events (wars, defense spending) create short-term price volatility but the long-term investment case for defense technology companies depends on sustained program adoption and TAM expansion
implicitAssumptions: Defense procurement cycles and technology adoption timelines are sufficiently long that short-term conflict resolution does not materially impact multi-year revenue trajectories
Successful battlefield demonstration of technologies like laser defense systems creates irreversible demand for procurement even if the immediate conflict ends
Government budget allocation to defense technology persists independently of specific conflict catalysts once strategic value is demonstrated

positions.claim: Near-term financial metrics (current profitability, legacy business drag, capex cycles) should be discounted when valuing companies positioned for structural demand inflections in 2027-2028
implicitAssumptions: Market participants systematically over-weight near-term earnings in valuation models relative to high-probability future inflections that are 2-3 years out
Companies can successfully execute massive capex buildouts and operational transitions without material execution risk or cost overruns
The timing of demand inflections (late 2027-2028) is predictable enough to justify holding through extended periods of poor near-term fundamentals

positions.claim: Market commentary focused on price action, charts, or sentiment without discussing specific operational metrics, catalysts, or governance changes lacks analytical credibility
implicitAssumptions: Narrative and sentiment analysis provides no incremental information beyond what can be derived from operational and financial fundamentals
Market participants who focus on price action are systematically wrong or uninformed rather than processing different information sets
Governance changes and operational metrics are always knowable and analyzable with sufficient research effort

positions.claim: Cost increases in energy and raw materials will be successfully passed through to hyperscalers who will continue battling for allocation rather than reducing demand
implicitAssumptions: Hyperscaler demand for AI infrastructure is price-inelastic within the range of plausible cost increases from energy and materials
Competition among hyperscalers for strategic positioning in AI creates a prisoner's dilemma that prevents coordinated demand reduction
Supply constraints are severe enough that suppliers maintain pricing power even as input costs rise

positions.claim: Portfolio concentration in top momentum sectors (Photonics, Memory, Power, Defense) and individual stock selection within those sectors is superior to diversification across broader technology categories
implicitAssumptions: Sector rotation risk is lower than stock-specific risk within validated high-momentum sectors experiencing structural demand shifts
The ability to identify which sectors will have sustained momentum is more reliable than the ability to predict individual stock performance within random sectors
Correlation within these sectors during drawdowns will not eliminate the benefits of individual stock selection
